question: Will CPI rise more than 0.2% in May 2026?
reasoning: The market question asks for May 2026 monthly CPI to rise more than 0.2% (seasonally adjusted). Recent CPI data shows February rose 0.3% and January rose 0.2%. While inflation forecasts show upward pressure from tariffs and other factors in 2026, the specific monthly threshold of >0.2% for May creates a high bar. Long Forecast predicts declining CPI rates through May 2026, and multiple expert analyses suggest peak inflation pressures may occur earlier in Q2 2026, potentially before May.
key_factors: Recent monthly CPI readings: February +0.3%, January +0.2%
Tariff passthrough effects expected to peak in Q2 2026
Energy price headwinds from geopolitical tensions
Housing inflation still elevated but showing signs of moderation
Seasonal adjustment factors may influence May reading
risks: Unpredictable oil price spikes from Iran conflict
Faster-than-expected tariff passthrough to consumer prices
Data revisions could affect historical comparisons
Supply chain disruptions could drive food/goods inflation
Methodological changes in CPI weighting for 2026
